headline: Press Freedom Ranking Falls 8 Places to 115th Globally
context: RSF dropped Malaysia 8 places to 115th in 2025 World Press Freedom Index citing prosecutions and media licensing.

cards.big: Malaysia drops 8 places to 115th in 2025 press freedom rankings
cards.sub: Prosecutions media licensing and MCMC powers drove the decline

cards.big: 12 journalists faced criminal charges for their reporting in 2025
cards.sub: Charges included sedition defamation and CMA violations

cards.big: 2 journalists detained under SOSMA for reporting on defence procurement
cards.sub: Detention lasted 28 days before release without charges

cards.big: Malaysia ranks below Philippines at 109th and Indonesia at 98th
cards.sub: Only Vietnam Myanmar and Laos rank lower in Southeast Asia

cards.big: Press freedom decline tests whether journalism can function under increasing legal pressure
Press freedom ranking fell 8 places to 115th. Rankings measure perception, but the trend is the signal.
